name: QRadar-0006 - Windows Privilege Escalation Detection
description: Detects privilege escalation attempts on Windows systems by monitoring for suspicious use of built-in elevation tools, UAC bypass techniques, and unexpected additions to privileged groups.

mitre.technique_name: Abuse Elevation Control Mechanism: Bypass User Account Control

detection.log_source_type: Windows Security Event Log, Windows System Event Log, Sysmon
detection.event_name_filter: Group membership change, Special logon, Explicit credentials
detection.aql: SELECT username, sourceIP, qid, CATEGORYNAME(category) as event_category, COUNT(*) as event_count FROM events WHERE LOGSOURCETYPENAME(devicetype) ILIKE '%windows%' AND (QIDDESCRIPTION(qid) ILIKE '%added to%' OR QIDDESCRIPTION(qid) ILIKE '%privilege%' OR QIDDESCRIPTION(qid) ILIKE '%administrator%' OR UTF8(payload) ILIKE '%whoami /priv%' OR UTF8(payload) ILIKE '%bypassuac%' OR UTF8(payload) ILIKE '%runas%' OR UTF8(payload) ILIKE '%net localgroup%') GROUP BY username, sourceIP, qid, event_category HAVING COUNT(*) >= 1 LAST 15 MINUTES

response.offense_description: Suspicious privilege escalation activity detected. User may have been added to a privileged group or UAC bypass was attempted.

notes: Inspired by SIGMA rules: win_susp_uac_bypass. Uses QIDDESCRIPTION instead of EventID for cross-version QRadar compatibility.
author: Security Team
